svg.desc: Component: Chat Page — Desktop Full Layout Page: /sessions/:id Design system version: 3.6 (2026-03-23) Artboards: desktop 1440px Closes: D-001 (partial — desktop frame with Memory panel open)

g.text: Can you explain how transformer attention mechanisms work
and why they're more effective than RNNs for sequence modeling?
Transformer attention allows every token to directly attend to every other token in the
sequence simultaneously. Unlike RNNs, which process tokens one-by-one and compress context
into a fixed hidden state, attention retains full contextual access regardless of sequence length.
Based on the research I found, several key papers converge on the same insight: the
quadratic scaling of standard attention (O(n²)) is the central computational challenge,
which FlashAttention addresses through IO-aware tiling
What about the computational complexity of attention?
